what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note that records Tim's point about adding an explicit per-envelope assignee and status lifecycle, and about routing typed comments/annotations to a supervisor or AI for triage.
observations: YAML frontmatter gives id, board:// address, type note, source claude_code, author_session tim, channel dragnet-development, and a commented_on link to item-bb66a021
Created and updated timestamps are identical, with a single history entry marking it filed
Body is tagged [point] and references a prior item about explicit per-envelope assignee + status lifecycle
Proposes a named lifecycle: received → triaged → working → replied → applied/closed
Mentions this is the breakdown's BD-D/BD-F status idea and invokes Linear's discipline of making every comment-that-implies-work a trackable item with one owner and one status
Suggests typed comments/annotations stored as values in a registry, routed to particular places based on type
Some types would route to a supervisor for assessment/escalation; general ones would route to an AI to decide next steps
Author expresses uncertainty about how the AI would be given an enumerated list or make decisions
standouts: Explicit per-envelope assignee plus a named status lifecycle
Typed comments/annotations as registry values that drive routing
Author's own uncertainty about the AI decision mechanism
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter, board:// address, link kind commented_on, and bracketed body tag [point]
Informal, first-person prose with speculative asides
concerns: The AI triage mechanism is hand-wavy and lacks concrete rules or enumerated list
Mixes a firm design proposal with unresolved uncertainty about implementation
No acceptance criteria or examples of annotation types
notes: Tim's note arguing for a Linear-style, owner-assigned, status-tracked envelope lifecycle and a typed annotation registry that routes comments to a supervisor or AI.
questions: What exactly are BD-D and BD-F?
What is the schema and initial set of values for the annotation type registry?
How does the supervisor decide whether to escalate?
What enumerated list or decision framework would the AI use?
What is the content of the linked item-bb66a021?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to make a point about explicit assignee/status lifecycle and typed routing; the body matches that purpose while also voicing uncertainty about AI triage.
intention_signals: The note is trying to formalize a work-tracking discipline so that every comment implying work has an owner, a status, and a routing path, using typed annotations to automate triage.
novelty: It combines a per-envelope status lifecycle with a typed annotation registry whose values route comments to either a supervisor or an AI decision-maker.
contribution: Keeps the design idea that every envelope/comment gets an assignee and a status, and that annotation types in a configurable registry determine routing to a supervisor or AI triage step.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development proposes an explicit per-envelope assignee and status lifecycle (received→triaged→working→replied→applied/closed), with typed comments/annotations stored in a registry and routed to a supervisor or AI for triage and escalation.
intention_for_embedding: To argue for Linear-style trackable ownership and status for every comment-that-implies-work, using typed annotations to automate routing.
